threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still use the Tube for everything. The locals know you can walk from Dalston Junction to most places faster than waiting for a train.
Saw a bloke on the 333 last night serenading a girl with a ukulele—she was trying to pretend she wasn’t listening but the whole bus was in on it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid riding a bike with a pet chicken in the basket down Sonnenallee. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here do you find Späti owners refusing to sell you a beer after 2 AM, citing 'not tonight, too many drunkards'—it's oddly charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can name at least three taco trucks that definitely don’t take cards—no cash, no tacos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 at 6 PM, no seats, but a guy juggling flaming torches while we rolled past city hall. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss how the locals really love their vermut hour; it’s not just a drink, it’s the social glue of the whole community.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they enthusiastically order a ‘café con leche’ and wait for it to arrive, like it’s a fancy drink.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'next stop!' half a block before you expect it. Trust me, we all used to.
Saw a man serenading the night bus crowd with a ukulele at Clapham South. Almost took the whole ride to work out the chords but got a free show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was truly a Neuköllner when I jumped on a bus that didn't arrive for 30 minutes, but everyone around me was casually chatting like it was normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you hear techno beats blaring from the park while kids play in the sun. It's a wild mix of chaos and calm that feels just right.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to the BART delays; they shrug it off and just order a burrito instead of panicking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 Geary today - a saxophonist played smooth jazz while the bus swayed. People stopped scrolling for a moment. Pure San Francisco magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has tapas, but locals know a good vermut is what you really want at 12 on a Sunday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they stop at every corner to snap photos of doors and colorful tiles—locals just walk past them.
